title: High Protein Sheet Pan Salmon
metaDescription: Sheet pan salmon with asparagus and cherry tomatoes — 36g of protein per serving, one pan, ready in 25 minutes with almost no cleanup.
author: Recipe Team
keywords: sheet pan salmon, salmon and asparagus, high protein dinner
ingredients: Fresh dill or parsley, for serving

steps.name: Preheat and prep
steps.text: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a sheet pan with parchment paper.

steps.name: Arrange everything
steps.text: Place salmon fillets on the sheet pan and surround with asparagus and cherry tomatoes. Drizzle everything with olive oil, minced garlic, salt, and pepper. Tuck lemon slices around the pan.

steps.name: Roast
steps.text: Roast for 12-15 minutes, until salmon flakes easily with a fork and reaches an internal temperature of 145°F.

steps.name: Serve
steps.text: Top with fresh dill or parsley and serve straight from the pan.

tips.question: How do I know when the salmon is done?
tips.answer: It should flake easily with a fork and reach an internal temperature of 145°F — pull it a minute early if you prefer it slightly more tender in the center, since it keeps cooking briefly off the heat.

tips.question: Can I use frozen salmon?
tips.answer: Yes, but thaw it fully in the fridge overnight first — cooking from frozen on a sheet pan leads to uneven results with the vegetables.

tips.question: What other vegetables work on this sheet pan?
tips.answer: Broccoli, green beans, or bell peppers all roast well alongside salmon — just cut them to a similar size as the asparagus for even cooking.

tips.question: Why did my asparagus turn out mushy?
tips.answer: Overcrowding the pan is the most common cause — the vegetables need room for the oven's heat to circulate and roast rather than steam. Use two pans if needed.

images.heroAlt: Sheet pan with roasted salmon, asparagus spears, cherry tomatoes and lemon slices
images.textureAlt: Fork flaking into a roasted salmon fillet revealing the moist, flaky interior
images.pinAlt: Fork flaking apart a roasted salmon fillet on a sheet pan with asparagus and tomatoes
